exemplar.title: Modelling an equation for a cooling kettle

detailedExemplarData.title: Modelling an equation for a cooling kettle

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The report is exceptionally coherent and logically developed, with each section flowing naturally into the next.
Headings, sub-headings and page breaks create a clear macro-structure that guides the reader.
Nearly all material is directly relevant, keeping the narrative focused on modeling kettle cooling.
weaknesses: Occasional verbosity and minor digressions (family anecdotes) slightly reduce conciseness.
Figure captions are sometimes too minimal and appendix tables are awkwardly split.
Some repeated algebraic steps could be tightened.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although minor lapses in conciseness remain, the exploration is exceptionally well organized, highly coherent and logically developed throughout. Headings and clear sign-posting give the report an introduction, body and conclusion that meet the highest descriptors for presentation.

strengths: Mathematical terminology and notation are used consistently and accurately throughout.
A broad range of representations (tables, scatter plots, fitted curves, linearised plots, error-analysis tables) are used effectively.
Arguments follow a clear logical sequence, with symbolic work always accompanied by prose explanation.
weaknesses: A few graphs lack full legends or scale labels, which could hinder immediate interpretation.
Minor slips (e.g. omitting the variable in one model instance) appear but do not affect overall clarity.
Some column headings in tables are cramped and missing unit labels.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite a handful of small presentation issues, the student makes highly effective and mostly consistent use of mathematical language, notation and multiple representations. The logical structure and clarity of communication justify the top band for mathematical communication.

strengths: The personal context and narrative voice in the introduction show genuine engagement and motivation.
The choice to compare three distinct models with error metrics demonstrates significant initiative.
Clear justifications of data-collection choices reflect an authentic personal approach.
weaknesses: The investigative techniques and mathematical methods remain standard to the syllabus.
Independent thinking is evident but does not reach an outstanding or highly innovative level.
The structure of the exploration follows familiar textbook exemplars.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates a clear personal approach and significant independent thinking, but the methods and structure stay within common course conventions, placing the work firmly in band 2 for personal engagement.

strengths: The student provides meaningful reflection on parameter choices and model performance.
Limitations (ambient temperature fluctuations, insulation effects) are clearly identified and linked to the aim.
Discussion of environmental influences shows thoughtful but primarily descriptive analysis.
weaknesses: Reflection lacks deeper quantitative or statistical analysis (e.g. sensitivity testing).
Justification for certain modelling decisions (choice of data points) is not fully explained.
Future-study suggestions are present but not developed into comprehensive experimental plans.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration contains thoughtful, meaningful reflection and a clear evaluation of outcomes, but does not extend to the deep critical analysis or comprehensive future-implication discussion required for band 3.

strengths: Mathematical techniques (exponential decay models, linearisation, first-order differential equations) are used expertly throughout.
Parameter estimation and error analysis are integrated seamlessly to answer the research question.
The choice and control of variables (e.g. standardizing volume) demonstrate a deep understanding of model applicability.
weaknesses: A few minor notation issues appear (missing t in one exponent, unit labels for k).
Ambient temperature is taken as given without documented measurement methodology.
Some rounding inconsistencies occur in intermediate arithmetic.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The depth and breadth of mathematical contentâespecially the seamless integration of calculus, logarithmic linearisation, and error metricsâgo well beyond good SL-level work. The student exhibits comprehensive, expert application of mathematics in service of the investigation.

strandSelections.reasoning:
Arguments are set out in a logical sequence, and each symbolic manipulation is followed by prose explanation, giving good clarity.  Occasional algebra is repeated unnecessarily and some steps (e.g.
$60-21=0.027t$) contain minor transcription errors, so the communication is âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ.

Algebraic manipulation, linearisation, and differential-equation solution are mostly correct; the student correctly derives *k* values and evaluates logarithms.  Minor slips (missing *t* in one formula, rounding inconsistencies) are present but do not affect conclusions, so understanding is good and accuracy mostly correct, consistent with band 4.

The student employs a data table, scatter plot, three fitted curves, a linearised plot, and summary tables of mean error.  These multiple representations are appropriate and generally well-chosen.  Axes are labelled, but some graphs lack scale numbers or clear legends, preventing the work from achieving the âhighly effectiveâ level.

The student structures the narrative chronologically: introduction â data collection â modelling attempts â comparison â evaluation.  Each section flows into the next and the mathematical development (algebraic model â linearised model â differential-equation model) is easy to follow, showing clear logical progression.  Minor lapses, such as occasional digressions about family anecdotes, do not break the overall flow.

Links between paragraphs are generally explicit (e.g. âThe next step wasâ¦â, âOnce more, the graphs appear similarâ¦â), so the reader is rarely left guessing why a step was taken.  However, passages are sometimes verbose and the algebraic work is occasionally repeated, preventing the work from reaching the âhighly coherent / excellentâ descriptor.

Consequently the exploration attains the descriptor "coherent and logically developed" rather than the highest level.

The student shows creativity by translating Newtonâs Law into a practical experiment and by comparing three self-derived models with an error metric.  However, the work does not introduce novel mathematics or unusual investigative techniques that would evidence exceptional initiative.

The student analyses residuals via mean absolute error and interprets why Equation 2 outperforms the others, demonstrating thoughtful but not deep analysis.  Broader statistical tests or sensitivity analysis are absent, so the criterion sits comfortably in band 2.

Outcomes are evaluated in the âEvaluationâ section, identifying limitations (variable room temperature, kettle insulation) and suggesting future improvements.  The evaluation is clear and linked to the aim, but does not extend to comprehensive quantitative validation, thus meeting but not exceeding band 2.

Reflection appears throughout: reasons for parameter choices, discussion of why each model over/under-estimates, and acknowledgement of environmental influences.  These comments are meaningful yet remain descriptive rather than deeply critical, matching the âmeaningful reflection throughoutâ band.

Mathematical terminology (ambient temperature, asymptote, exponential decay, differential equation) and notation (subscripts, natural logarithm, derivatives) are used correctly and almost consistently.  A few slips occur â e.g. omitting the variable *t* in the final Newton model or typing âln(e)=1â without clarification â but these are minor.  Hence use of language is âmostly consistentâ, meeting band 3.

Personal voice is clear in the rationale, in the justification of data-collection choices, and in reflections on family tea habits, indicating a clear personal approach.  It does not reach âhighly personal and innovativeâ because the modelling techniques and structure closely follow textbook exemplars.

Nearly all material included is relevant to modelling kettle cooling; redundancies (extended family story, repetition of equation rearrangements) add length but do not dominate.  At ~14 pages of main text the IA is within limits yet could still be tightened, so it is âconcise with mostly relevant contentâ rather than âhighly concise with all content relevant.â

All mathematics used (exponential decay, logarithmic linearisation, differential equation solution, error analysis) is directly relevant to modelling the kettleâs cooling.  Each technique contributes to answering the research question, illustrating effective use, though the repertoire is not broad enough to be called âcomprehensiveâ or âexpertâ.

The choice of context originates from the studentâs personal experience and the decision to test several modelling strategies shows meaningful initiative beyond a template.  Nevertheless, the mathematics applied is standard to the course and the comparisons are straightforward, so the evidence of truly independent or original thinking is âsignificantâ but not âoutstandingâ.

Headings, sub-headings, and page breaks give the report a clear macro-structure (Introduction, Data Collection, Initial Graphing, Calculations, Evaluation, Appendix).  Tables and graphs are placed close to the text that refers to them, and the appendix is sign-posted correctly.

Despite this good organisation, figure captions are sometimes minimal (e.g. âGraph 4â) and the appendix tables are awkwardly split, so navigation is not flawless.  Still, the overall structure comfortably meets the âwell-organised with clear structureâ description.

The mathematics is fully commensurate with SL AA expectations: use of natural logs, solving first-order separable differential equations, and parameter estimation.  While appropriate and well applied, it does not push beyond syllabus level (e.g. no calculus-based optimisation or advanced statistics) and therefore aligns with band 4.

annotations.comment: Consider discussing the precision and potential error of the kettleâs built-in display to strengthen reflection on measurement reliability and data accuracy.
Table headings are cramped and lack clear unit labels. Reformat column headings and include units prominently to improve clarity.
The âInitial graphingâ heading frames the section well, but a short introductory sentence outlining the next analytical steps would enhance the reportâs organization.
Graph 1 omits scale numbers and a legend distinguishing the asymptote. Ensure axes have clear tick-labels and legends for all plotted elements.
The caption for Graph 1 is minimal. Expand it to summarise key observations (e.g. rate of decay, room-temperature asymptote) for better communication.
The initial model T=e^(ât) omits both ambient temperature and amplitude parameters. Include Ta and k from the outset to clarify your modelâs structure.
Ta is stated as 24 Â°C without measured support. Provide a room-temperature reading or explain the method used for this value.
The choice of base e is appropriate; referencing its role in solving differential equations would strengthen the mathematical communication.
The personal context in the introduction effectively demonstrates genuine motivation and makes the investigation highly personal, engaging the reader with clear independent thinking.
The extended family kettle anecdote, while engaging, digresses from the mathematical focus. Consider tightening this narrative to maintain relevance and conciseness.
The aim clearly states the use of exponential functions and Newtonâs Law of Cooling, providing coherence between theory and the planned approach.
Standardising the volume to one litre demonstrates thoughtful methodological control, ensuring consistency in cooling rate comparisons.
The linearisation narrative is effective but verbose. Summarise the core transformation steps more concisely to improve flow and relevance.
The mapping to y=mx+c is well executed; explicitly state which variable corresponds to y and which to x to avoid ambiguity.
Equation 2 is introduced before the mean-error method is explained. Briefly link the model derivation to the upcoming error analysis for coherence.
Defining k as a positive constant is correct; discuss its physical meaning (cooling rate) to deepen readers' conceptual understanding.
When integrating the differential equation, explicitly annotate the integration variables (dT, dt) on both sides to reinforce mathematical clarity.
The model T=24+(100â24)e^(âkt) is solved for k properly, but the next equation omits the variable t in the exponent; correct it to e^(â0.035t).
Your discussion of environmental influences on Equation 3 shows substantive reflection. For deeper insight, quantify air-temperature changes around the kettle if possible.
Your time calculation mixes methods: you derive t via ln((60â24)/79)/(â0.037) but then write â60â21=0.027tâ. Reconcile both to maintain consistency.
The evaluation thoroughly identifies insulation and ambient-temperature limitations. Including design improvements (e.g. thermally insulated vessel) would strengthen future-study suggestions.
The discussion of convection, evaporation, and radiation shows good reflection depth. Linking these factors to an extended model would enhance the critical analysis.
The appendix table uses a decorative â¤ for the zero-difference entry, which may confuse readers. Use standard numeric notation consistently.
Consider adding a brief summary statement of the mean absolute error below the appendix table to help readers quickly interpret model accuracy.
The step âln(e)=1â is correct but unmotivated. Add a brief note that ln(e)=1 to guide readers unfamiliar with logarithmic properties.
You set up simultaneous ln(TâTa) equations at t=0 and t=65 but do not justify choosing t=65. Explain why this point was selected or use evenly spaced data points.
The derivation of a is logical but the arithmetic ââ2.25/â65â is approximated. Show the intermediate ln(8/76) value for transparency.
T=24+76e^(â0.034t) is clear, but stating that k has units of min^â1 would reinforce understanding of its physical interpretation.
